title: The Gaelic Athlete, vol. VI, no. 141, Saturday September 12th 1914

Connacht was submerged last Sunday
when the opposition of Munster was en-
countered in the All-Ireland Senior Semi-

finals. Cork administered a very heavy
defeat to the Galway hurlers, who were
never in the picture. The football bout
between Kerry and Roscommon seemed to
possess some interesting possibilities at the
outset, but Kerry's facile knack of obtain
ing. major scores knocked the Western
cchances'on the head. Connacht has sill
a [ot of leeway to make up to reach the
Munster and Leinster standards. Even
Ulster—the erstwhile Cinderella of the
G.A.A—is now her superior in football,

Which naturally reminds us thar Ulster

‘on next Sunday at Cork Park, The new

rose up, upon the grand stand at a league

In any case iti quite likely that the football

A SURVEY.

By “ QuruooKer”

Next Sunday igthe footballers’ day out.
Struggles of momentous importance ate
fixed for Dublin and Dungarvan which
will probably do more than anything yet

Great Expectations

Great things ate expected from Mona.
ghan this season. ‘They have proved them-
selves unquestionably superior to all oppo-
sition in Ulster, and they are fully qualified
to represent the province. In their matches
with Decry, Armagh. Fermanagh, etc.
they showed themselves a combination with
reat possibilities. Furthermore, their mast
recent matches have been engaged in with
the minimum amount of change in the
personnel of the players. For example, the
team which. will-;eprevent them anSunday
next only shows one change from the team
which contested the Ulster final, and this
change is brought about by the inability of
a player to travel.. They have every conf
dence in their, ability to give Wexford a
tun for the, honours, which they (Wex:
ford) are not likely to forget. When they
take the field they do so with a determina
tion to-concede nothing that human effort
can possibly withhold, Animated by a
spirit like this, they should certainly provide
aan hour of thrills at Croke Park on Sunday
next,

ford already that very little can be added
‘without going over old. ground. In their

HARP CYCLE CLUB

25 MILES ROAD RAGE

‘An impoctant meeting of the above club
wat held at 46-Rathaioes Road on the 7th
September, ‘Arrangements were completed
for the 25 miles eye'e race to be held on
the Nass Road. This evest_ will be run

with a standard gold medal, and the club
will provide the other “pots.” A record

Valuable Silver Cup and Standard Gald

and prize-Gold centee medal. 3¢d prize

FARRONFORE

A clever sprinter in the person of W. J.
Foley of Tralee won the final of the century
at this meeting from a good field. Brassil,
his fellow-townsman, annexed the 220,
while Murphy of Currow outsted Brassil
in the 440 by about 3 yards. J. J. Mah-
foney won the mile fat after a very hot
finish. ‘T, E, Kelly obtained first place in
the Long Jump, and first pli

Hop Step and Jump. The cycling events
were very briskly contested and on the whole
showed an equal division of honours, T.
Vaughan, Tralee, won the mile, M.
Milner, Tralee, the two miles, while
M, Jonies won the three miles. Between
these three cyclists all the second and third
places were divided.

points. Although the exchanges were
ftrenuowsly contested, the winners’ sup

city and cleverness’ was always vid
Shauns won by theic more scientific

individually being streets ahead of the op~
position, Shauns were at times rather

severely hustled about, their opponents, if

ling and vigorous tactics at close quarters.

‘The Stephens played far better than in pre-
For the winners, Connolly in goal was

very reliable. Rea and Shechan tackled

wards, 2
